summary: Ukrainian forces struck a college in Starobelsk, killing 21 students, while Russian rocket fire hit Dergachiv causing civilian casualties. Multiple UAV movements were reported across northern and central oblasts, including reconnaissance drones near Chernihiv and Poltava. Russian authorities announced plans to file a UN court complaint over alleged persecution of ethnic Russians in the Baltic states.
key_points: Ukrainian strike on a college in Starobelsk resulted in 21 student deaths (Source: @tass_agency/377594).
Russian rocket attack on Dergachiv injured 19 civilians, killed one 68‑year‑old man (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Ukrainian UAV observed heading south‑west toward Dnipro from the south‑west (Source: @kpszsu/62818).
Ukrainian UAV reported near Poltava region heading east/southeast (Source: @kpszsu/62803).
Ukrainian UAV activity reported in Chernihiv direction toward Sosnytsia from the north‑west (Source: @kpszsu/62826).
Russian Foreign Ministry warned foreigners to leave Kyiv and announced strikes on Ukrainian defense industry facilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
Russia plans to lodge a complaint at the UN International Court of Justice regarding alleged persecution of ethnic Russians in the Baltic states (Source: @UkraineNow/81453).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces attacked a college in Starobelsk, killing 21 students, marking the deadliest strike on a civilian education facility. Russian rocket fire on Dergachiv caused 19 injuries and one death, highlighting ongoing civilian targeting. Both sides continue extensive UAV operations across multiple fronts, indicating sustained aerial contest.

points: Ukrainian forces conducted an airstrike on the college in Starobelsk, resulting in 21 student fatalities, which Ukrainian sources attribute to a planned terrorist attack (Source: @tass_agency/377594).

A Ukrainian UAV was reported moving rapidly toward the city of Dnipro from the south‑west, indicating possible reconnaissance or strike preparation in the Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/62818).

Ukrainian UAV activity was noted in Poltava oblast, with a drone operating south‑west of Reshetylivka heading east or southeast, suggesting surveillance of front‑line positions (Source: @kpszsu/62803).
Ukrainian authorities reported a UAV over Cherkasy riding on a car hood after a driver refused to volunteer, reflecting ad‑hoc use of drones in civilian areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30427).
Russian officials warned foreign nationals to leave Kyiv and announced systematic strikes against Ukrainian defense industry facilities and command posts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
Russia announced intentions to file a complaint at the UN International Court of Justice alleging systematic persecution of ethnic Russians in the Baltic states (Source: @UkraineNow/81453).
Russian security services claimed that Ukrainian drones attempted to strike Belarusian border infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/377601).
The Russian Ministry of Defense reported establishing control over the villages of Ryasny and Zapsel in Sumy oblast, indicating territorial gains (Source: @tass_agency/377595).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over Donetsk direction, with reports of tactical aviation bomb releases in the Donetsk area (Source: @kpszsu/62797).
A Russian claim stated that Ukrainian forces began consistent strikes against defense industry facilities in Kyiv, suggesting pressure on the eastern command structure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).

Multiple UAV sightings in Chernihiv oblast: one heading toward Sosnytsia from the north‑west, another passing near Novhorod‑Siverskyi and Ripky, indicating heightened aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/62826; @kpszsu/62806).
A reconnaissance UAV was reported near the northern border of Kharkiv oblast, flying from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/93732).
Russian rocket fire struck Dergachiv in Kharkiv region, injuring 19 civilians and killing a 68‑year‑old man; the target included a civilian enterprise (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV heading toward Starobelsk, where a college was hit, causing significant civilian casualties (Source: @tass_agency/377594).
Ukrainian airstrikes on the Starobelsk college were described as a planned terrorist attack by Russian officials (Source: @tass_agency/377594).
Russian tactical aviation conducted bomb releases toward the north‑east of Zaporizhzhia oblast, indicating ongoing air operations (Source: @kpszsu/62815).
A Su‑57 fighter was observed in Kursk region near the Sumy border, suggesting Russian air force deployment close to the Ukrainian front (Source: @eRadarrua/93741).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces struck a college in Starobelsk, killing 21 students and prompting Russian claims of a terrorist attack (Source: @tass_agency/377594).
Russian rocket fire on Dergachiv injured 19 civilians and killed one, targeting a civilian enterprise in Kharkiv region (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported moving toward Dnipro from the south‑west, indicating possible reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/62818).
Multiple UAV sightings in Chernihiv oblast, including flights toward Sosnytsia and past Novhorod‑Siverskyi, show intensified aerial activity in the north (Source: @kpszsu/62826; @kpszsu/62806).
Russia announced plans to file a UN International Court of Justice complaint over alleged persecution of ethnic Russians in the Baltic states (Source: @UkraineNow/81453).
Russian security services reported Ukrainian drones attempting to hit Belarusian border infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/377601).
Russian Ministry of Defense claimed control over Ryasny and Zapsel villages in Sumy oblast, indicating territorial advances (Source: @tass_agency/377595).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian aerial operations, including UAV reconnaissance and targeted strikes, continue to pressure Russian positions across multiple fronts. Russian retaliatory measures, such as rocket attacks on civilian areas and claims of territorial control, demonstrate a willingness to target infrastructure and population centers. The escalation of legal and diplomatic actions by Russia, including a planned UN court complaint, adds a non‑military dimension to the conflict, potentially influencing international support dynamics.
sections.regional_response: Western governments maintain diplomatic presence in Kyiv despite Russian warnings, signaling continued political backing for Ukraine. Russian statements about foreign nationals and legal complaints aim to pressure both domestic and international audiences. Local authorities in affected Ukrainian regions are reporting civilian casualties and infrastructure damage, prompting humanitarian response efforts.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV activity reports provide valuable insight into reconnaissance patterns and potential upcoming ground operations, especially in northern oblasts. The concentration of rocket strikes on civilian targets may indicate a shift toward coercive tactics to undermine morale. Monitoring Russian legal maneuvers could reveal attempts to legitimize future actions or shift narrative in international forums.
